title: crepis vesicaria subsp. bivonana
intro: Crepis vesicaria subsp. bivonana, also known as Bivon's hawk's-beard, is a subspecies of the Crepis vesicaria plant, a member of the dandelion family Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Crepis vesicaria subsp. bivonana has been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, skin conditions, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are made by infusing the plant's leaves and roots in a solvent or by mixing it with other herbs.
